terms.definition: An open-source AI agent that runs locally and connects LLMs to external tools through Skills
terms.example: e.g. The session's `aws-health` skill lets OpenClaw answer "check my AWS health" via WhatsApp

terms.definition: A self-contained unit of agent capability made of one folder containing a SKILL.md file plus any scripts it references
terms.example: e.g. The `aws-health` skill folder contains SKILL.md, package.json, and scripts/aws-health.ts

terms.definition: The instruction file inside a skill that tells the agent its trigger phrases, invocation command, output format, and error handling
terms.example: e.g. SKILL.md tells the agent to run `npx tsx scripts/aws-health.ts` when a user asks about AWS health

terms.definition: The OpenClaw process that connects the agent to a chat channel and routes incoming messages to the correct skill
terms.example: e.g. `openclaw gateway restart` reloads the gateway after editing openclaw.json

terms.definition: The conversational OpenClaw agent that actually executes skills, distinct from the configuration agent
terms.example: e.g. A user must run `talk to agent` to reach the main agent before a skill will trigger

terms.definition: The current modular version of the AWS JavaScript/TypeScript SDK, split into per-service client packages
terms.example: e.g. `@aws-sdk/client-ec2`, `@aws-sdk/client-ecs`, and `@aws-sdk/client-cloudwatch` are used in this skill

terms.definition: A TypeScript execution tool that runs .ts files directly without a separate compile step
terms.example: e.g. `npx tsx scripts/aws-health.ts` runs the script with no build step

terms.definition: AWS's resizable virtual server (compute) service
terms.example: e.g. The skill reports instance type, state, and CPU usage for every EC2 instance

terms.definition: AWS's container orchestration service for running Docker containers as tasks and services
terms.example: e.g. The skill lists every cluster and reports running vs desired task counts per service

terms.definition: AWS's monitoring and observability service used to collect and retrieve metrics, logs, and alarms
terms.example: e.g. The skill calls CloudWatch's GetMetricStatistics to compute a 15-minute CPU average

terms.definition: An EC2 API call that returns metadata and current state for EC2 instances
terms.example: e.g. Used to list instance name, type, AZ, and running/stopped state

terms.definition: An EC2 API call that returns system and instance status checks
terms.example: e.g. Used to detect underlying hardware failures separate from OS-level problems

terms.definition: An EC2 health check that detects problems with the underlying AWS hardware/infrastructure, as opposed to the guest OS
terms.example: e.g. A failed system status check usually means AWS needs to migrate the instance off failing hardware

terms.definition: An ECS API call that returns running/desired task counts and deployment state for a service
terms.example: e.g. Shows "3/3 desired" when a service is fully healthy

terms.definition: An ECS API call returning detail on individual ECS tasks, including stop reason and stop code
terms.example: e.g. Used to list the last 5 stopped tasks and why each one stopped

terms.definition: The ECS-reported status of a service deployment, either COMPLETED, IN_PROGRESS, or FAILED
terms.example: e.g. A FAILED rollout state signals the new task definition never reached a healthy state

terms.definition: A CloudWatch API call used to retrieve aggregated metric data such as a CPU average over a time window
terms.example: e.g. The skill calls it to compute the 15-minute average CPU utilization per instance

terms.definition: AWS's Identity and Access Management service for controlling who or what can call which APIs
terms.example: e.g. The skill authenticates as a dedicated IAM user named openclaw-bot

terms.definition: The security principle of granting only the exact permissions required to do a job, nothing more
terms.example: e.g. The skill's IAM policy contains only Describe/List/Get actions, never write actions

terms.definition: An IAM policy restricted to non-mutating actions like Describe*, List*, and Get*
terms.example: e.g. OpenClawAWSHealthReadOnly grants only those three action prefixes

terms.term: Access Key ID / Secret Access Key
terms.definition: The credential pair used to authenticate an IAM user programmatically against AWS APIs
terms.example: e.g. These two values are set as environment variables for the skill to authenticate

terms.definition: An additional environment variable required only when using temporary, assumed-role credentials
terms.example: e.g. Left unset for a standard long-lived IAM user, but required when assuming a role via STS

terms.definition: A pattern where a container repeatedly starts and stops or crashes shortly after starting
terms.example: e.g. Visible in ECS as several recently stopped tasks sharing the same stop reason

terms.definition: An AWS service reporting account- and service-level operational issues, distinct from per-resource Describe calls
terms.example: e.g. It would tell you "ECS is degraded in ap-south-1" but not which specific service of yours is unhealthy

terms.definition: An SDK error indicating missing or invalid AWS credentials were supplied to a client
terms.example: e.g. Thrown when AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID or AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY is unset

terms.definition: An AWS API error indicating the calling identity lacks the IAM permission required for that action
terms.example: e.g. Thrown if the openclaw-bot user's policy is missing an ecs:DescribeServices action

terms.definition: An AWS API error indicating the requested resource, such as an ECS cluster, does not exist under that name
terms.example: e.g. Thrown when --cluster is passed a typo'd or nonexistent cluster name
